.

Statement Stencil

Statement of evidence of Garda ____________ of Mountjoy Garda Station
made on the ______________."I hereby declare that this statement is true
to the best of my knowledge and belief and I make it knowing that if it
is tendered in evidence I will be liable to prosecution if I state in it
anything which I know to be false or do not believe to be true." I am a
member of An Garda Síochána attached to Mountjoy Garda Station. On Day
the Date, I was on duty in uniform/plain clothes, assigned to (mobile
patrol/beat/specific duty) with Garda ____________ in patrol car
callsign ____________. At approximately TIME, I was directed by Command
& Control / observed / received a report of an incident at ____________,
a public place/private dwelling/premises. On arrival, I observed
____________ (describe circumstances, persons present, vehicles,
behaviour).

Complaint Options (delete as appropriate)

-   On DATE at TIME, ____________ attended at the public office of
    ____________ Garda Station and provided details of an incident which
    had occurred at ____________.

-   They outlined that ____________.

Environment / Scene Description (if applicable)

On arrival, I observed the environment of the location to be
____________.

-   The area was lit by ____________ (street lighting/indoor
    lighting/natural light) and weather conditions were ____________
    (clear/heavy rain/windy/dark).

-   The roadway/footpath/surroundings were ____________
    (quiet/busy/obstructed), and I noted the presence of ____________
    (vehicles, pedestrians, bystanders).

-   The premises/room/area itself appeared ____________
    (tidy/disordered/damaged), with ____________ (doors/windows open or
    broken, items disturbed, signs of struggle).

-   The overall atmosphere was ____________ (calm/hostile/chaotic), and
    I recorded these observations as relevant to the circumstances of
    the incident.

OR (if I did not attend the scene):

-   I did not personally attend the scene. My information in relation to
    the environment and condition of the location is based on accounts
    provided by ____________ (SOCO/attending Garda/witness).

Scenes of Crime

-   Scenes of Crime Officer (SOCO) attended the scene at TIME on DATE.

Circumstances Leading to Arrest

Based on the complaint received, witness accounts, and my own
observations, I formed the opinion that an arrestable offence had been
committed. In particular:

Witnesses stated that ____________.

-   CCTV footage/property recovered showed ____________.

-   Items consistent with the commission of the offence were found in
    the possession of ____________.

Having considered all of the above, I was satisfied that reasonable
grounds existed to suspect that ____________ had committed an arrestable
offence.

CCTV / Digital Evidence (if applicable)

-   At TIME, I seized a mobile phone make/model ____________, IMEI
    ____________, belonging to ____________, pursuant to Section 7 of
    the Criminal Justice Act 2006.

-   I believed that this device contained evidence relating to the
    offence for which ____________ was arrested / other related
    offences. The phone was packaged, sealed and entered into evidence
    as SMG***.

CCTV Options (delete as appropriate):

-   At TIME, I requested CCTV from ____________ pursuant to Section 41B
    of the Data Protection Act 2018.

-   The CCTV was provided to me on a USB/DVD by ____________ (name,
    role, e.g. store manager/security officer).

-   The footage covered multiple angles, including:

-   Camera 1 – located at ____________ (entrance, shop floor, corridor,
    cell block, etc.).

-   Camera 2 – located at ____________ (car park, street, rear door,
    etc.).

-   Camera 3 – located at ____________ (specify).

-   The cameras captured the accused entering/exiting /

committing the offence / interacting with the complainant.

-   The CCTV was sealed, labelled, and entered as exhibit SMG___.

Complainant / Witness Statements

-   A statement of complaint was taken from ____________ (name, DOB,
    address) at TIME on DATE.

-   Additional witness statements were taken from ____________.

Search (if applicable – Section Powers)

-   At TIME, I conducted a search of ____________
    (person/vehicle/premises) under Section ___ of the ____________ Act
    ____.

-   During the search, I recovered _________, which was packaged,
    sealed, labelled, and entered as exhibit SMG.

-   The search was witnessed by ____________.

Medical / Injury Notes (for assaults etc.)

-   I observed injuries to ____________, including ____________ (cuts,
    bruises, swelling).

-   They were conveyed to ____________ Hospital by
    ambulance/self/official Garda vehicle.

-   Medical report obtained from Dr ____________.

Domestic Violence (if applicable)

-   I confirmed that an order (Safety Order/Protection Order/Barring
    Order/Interim Order) number ____________ was in force.

-   The order was served on the respondent on DATE at TIME by Garda
    ____________.

Arrest

At TIME, I arrested ____________ of ____________ under Section ___ of
the ____________ Act ____, for the offence of ____________. I cautioned
____________ in ordinary language and they indicated that they did/did
not understand.

The arrested person was conveyed by official Garda vehicle, driven by
Garda ____________, to ____________ Garda Station, arriving at TIME.

On arrival, I introduced ____________ to the Gaoler, Garda ____________.
The Gaoler recorded the arrested person’s details in the custody record
pursuant to Regulation 7 of the Criminal Justice Act 1984 (Treatment of
Persons in Custody in Garda Síochána Stations) Regulations 1987.

Search on Reception

Following the recording of details, and in accordance with Regulation 17
of the Criminal Justice Act 1984 (Treatment of Persons in Custody in
Garda Síochána Stations) Regulations 1987, the arrested person was
searched by Garda ____________ in the presence of Garda ____________.

The purpose of this search was to ensure the safety of the arrested
person, members of An Garda Síochána, and others, and to secure and
preserve any property in their possession.

During the search, the following property was removed from the arrested
person: ____________. These items were recorded in the custody record
and stored in accordance

with procedure. No unauthorised articles/substances were found.

Regulation 8(1) – Information Given to Person in Custody

At TIME on DATE, in accordance with Regulation 8(1) of the Criminal
Justice Act 1984 (Treatment of Persons in Custody in Garda Síochána
Stations) Regulations 1987, I informed ____________ in ordinary language
of the reason for their detention and the offence for which they had
been arrested. They indicated that they did/did not understand.

Regulation 8(2) – Notice of Rights (C72)

At TIME, the arrested person was issued with a Notice of Rights (C72)
pursuant to Regulation 8(2) of the Criminal Justice Act 1984 (Treatment
of Persons in Custody in Garda Síochána Stations) Regulations 1987. They
signed/refused to sign in acknowledgement.

Section 4 CJA 1984 Detention Application & Authorisation

At TIME on DATE, I informed Sergeant ____________, Member-in-Charge, of
the facts of the case and applied to detain ____________ pursuant to
Section 4 of the Criminal Justice Act 1984, as amended.

I outlined that ____________ had been arrested for an offence contrary
to Section ___ of the ____________ Act ____. The grounds for detention
were based on the following evidence:

-   Complainant statement outlining the circumstances of the offence.

-   Garda observations at the scene, including the behaviour of the
    accused.

-   CCTV footage showing the accused in the vicinity at the relevant
    time.

-   Property and/or items seized and entered as exhibits (e.g.,
    controlled drugs, stolen items, weapons, mobile phones).

-   Witness accounts corroborating the complainant’s version of events.

I stated that detention was necessary for the proper investigation of
the offence, including:

-   Interviewing the accused regarding their involvement.

-   Obtaining an account of the accused’s movements.

-   Conducting forensic analysis (fingerprints, DNA, photos).

-   Establishing the identity of associates or accomplices.

Sergeant ____________, Member-in-Charge, considered my application and
at TIME on DATE authorised detention under Section 4(2) of the Criminal
Justice Act 1984.

The detained person was informed in plain language that they were being
detained pursuant to Section 4.

Interviews

Accused entered interview room at.

Manner / temperament.

Information by Sgt regarding recording of interviews to accused

Opened 3 blank dvds placed in player

Interview format, and questions and answers.

How was the suspect identified.

Q around Means motive and opportunity. Actus rea question topics men's
rea questions. Was there any extracts Reed from statements or footage
shown to the accused. Did the accused confirm their identity. What was
put to the accused how did they respond.

Who was present

Who asked the questions who wrote.

Exhibits. Cctv shown. Statements read out (extracts)

-   At TIME, I commenced an interview with ____________, under caution,
    at ____________ Garda Station, in the presence of Garda
    ____________. The interview was audio-recorded / video-recorded.

Visits from the member in charge?? Was it in around midnight, or what
there a rest period offered if so.

Consultations or interruptions from solicitor during the interview

Any requests for a water

Did you have to change DVDS.

Time terminated. Invitation to sign memo. Random pick of DVDS and
signing of the DVDS

Solicitor Options (delete as appropriate):

-   Prior to interview, the detained person consulted with solicitor
    ____________ by telephone.

-   Prior to interview, the detained person consulted with solicitor
    ____________, who attended in person and was present for the
    duration of the interview.

-   The solicitor was not present for the interview.

-   The detained person declined the offer of a solicitor and confirmed
    that they were willing to proceed without legal representation.

-   Add that the gaoler was informed of this by myself at...

Break / Re-attendance Option:

-   During the interview, solicitor ____________ left the room at TIME /
    re-attended at TIME. The interview continued in their
    absence/presence, with the detained person confirming that they were
    willing to proceed.

During interview, ____________ made admissions / denied involvement /
exercised their right to silence.

Solicitor / Medical (if applicable)

-   The arrested person requested / declined further consultation with a
    solicitor. Garda ____________ contacted solicitor ____________, who
    attended/telephoned.

-   Medical attention was offered. Dr ____________ attended at TIME and
    examined ____________.

Forensic Samples – Prints / DNA (if

applicable)

At TIME on DATE, I sought authorisation from Inspector ____________ of
____________ Garda Station under Section 11 of the Criminal Justice
(Forensic Evidence and DNA Database System) Act 2014 to take
fingerprints, palm prints and a DNA sample from ____________.

Inspector ____________ authorised same at TIME on DATE. I informed
____________ in ordinary language of the authorisation and of their
rights.

The procedure was conducted at TIME by Garda ____________
(fingerprints/palm prints) and Dr ____________ / Garda ____________
(buccal swab for DNA).

The following reference numbers were recorded:

-   Fingerprints: ____________

-   Palm prints: ____________

-   DNA swab kit seal number: ____________

All samples were packaged, sealed, and entered into evidence in
accordance with procedure.

Realesed from the provisions of the detention? Held for charge

Outcome

At TIME on DATE, ____________ was:

-   Charged by Sergeant ____________ with the offence of ____________,
    charge sheet number _______. They were cautioned after charge and
    made no reply / made reply

“***”.

-   Released on station bail with conditions to appear before
    ____________ District Court on DATE.

OR

-   Released pending a file to the Director of Public Prosecutions.

This statement has been read over by me and is correct.

Signed: Garda Sean McGreevy (40201L)

Interview information for investigating Garda statement

Interview Number / Location / Recording

Brought into into interview room, who was present in the room.

I confirmed the detained person’s fitness for interview (sober,
oriented, medical needs addressed).

Interview No.: 1 of ___ conducted in Interview Room ___ at Station.

Recording: Audio/Video recorded in accordance with the Criminal Justice
Act 1984 (Electronic Recording of Interviews) Regulations.

Solicitor: Attended in person / consulted by telephone at TIME /
declined. The detained person agreed / declined to proceed in the
absence of a solicitor.

Present: Garda ___ (lead questioner), Garda ___ (note-taker), detained
person ___ (DOB ___), solicitor ___ (present / not present / consulted
by phone), interpreter/appropriate adult ___ (if applicable).

.•Interpreter / Appropriate Adult arranged and present (name/agency
___), language ___. They confirmed accurate interpretation and
understanding.

Recording Notice on Entry (door closed)

At TIME, upon entry to Interview Room ___ and after the door was closed,
I informed [NAME] in ordinary language that:

The interview would be electronically recorded (audio/video) under the
Electronic Recording Regulations.

Three sealed blank DVDs would be used; at the end, one

would be selected by them as the Masterseam, and they would be invited
to select and sign the seals.

They did / did not indicate understanding.

Recording Media (three DVDs)

The seals were broken in the room and the discs were placed in the
recording device in view of those present. The device date/time was
announced on the record.

Any media change during the interview was announced; additional discs
SMG___(4)/(5) were opened as required and read onto the record.

Commenced at TIME on DATE.

Caution given: “You are not obliged to say anything unless you wish to
do so, but whatever you do say will be taken down and may be given in
evidence.” The detained person did / did not indicate understanding.

Start / End Times & Breaks

Breaks / Interruptions: (meal / rest / bathroom / solicitor consultation
/ media change / Member-in-Charge visit) at TIME–TIME. Detained person
agreed to resume each time.

Media changes: New DVD SMG___ opened at TIME and

announced on the record.

Identity & Preliminary Matters

The detained person confirmed / refused to confirm full name, DOB,
address, and that they are the person arrested at LOCATION at TIME for
OFFENCE contrary to SECTION/ACT.

Identification basis explained (e.g., CCTV Exhibit SMG___, witness ___,
recovered property SMG___).

Account Phase (Free Narrative)

Offered the opportunity to give a free, uninterrupted account of events
from START POINT to END POINT.

Follow-up questions covered: movements (times/locations), persons
present, means / motive / opportunity, property handled, communications
(calls/messages), and any alibi offered. Responses recorded verbatim /
“no comment” noted where given.

Challenge Phase (Proofs & Exhibits)

Elements of the offence were put and explored (actus reus / mens rea):

Actus reus — conduct, consequence, circumstances.

Mens rea — intent / knowledge / recklessness / dishonesty (as
applicable): “What did you intend?”, “What did you believe would
happen?”, “Why did you…?”

Exhibits put:

CCTV SMG___ played at –; the detained person identified / denied /
declined to view themselves/others. Response recorded.

Physical exhibits SMG___ (item) shown; chain briefly outlined; response
recorded.

Statement extracts (Witness ___) read and put;

response recorded.

Procedural Safeguards During Interview

No oppression, inducement, or unfair questioning occurred. The detained
person was / was not handcuffed; refreshments offered; conditions
appropriate.

Consultations: Solicitor attended / phoned at TIME; interview paused;
resumed at TIME.

Member-in-Charge attended the room at TIME (if applicable).

Closure & Media Sealing

The detained person was given a final opportunity to add, correct, or
clarify anything said. They did / did not add anything further.

Memo / Record: The memorandum of interview was read back / played back;
the detained person signed / refused to sign. Reasons for refusal (if
any) recorded.

Ended at TIME. Total interview time __ h __ m (excluding breaks).

Recording stopped; DVDs ejected in view of those present (Names)

The detained person was invited to select one DVD to be sealed as the
Master. They **selected disc no.

DVDs sealed and labelled as:

Master: (selected by detained person);

Seals signed by me, the detained person, and (where present) the
solicitor/interpreter. If any party declined to sign, this was recorded
with reasons.

Media lodged: Master to Property/Evidence, Working to Investigating
Member, Copy retained/managed per disclosure policy.

Interview Outcome (Summary)

During interview the detained person made admissions / partial
admissions / denied involvement / exercised the right to silence
regarding: [brief, factual summary of key points put and responses
given].

Assisting Garda Statement Stencil

Statement of Garda [FULL NAME], [REG. NO.] of [STATION], made on the
[DATE] at [STATION].

I hereby declare that this statement is true to the best of my knowledge
and belief and that I make it knowing if it is tendered in evidence I
will be liable to prosecution if I state in it anything which I know to
be false or do not believe to be true.

I am a member of An Garda Síochána and I am attached to [UNIT/SECTION]
based at [STATION].

On [DAY, DATE] at [TIME], I commenced duty at [STATION]. For the purpose
of this tour of duty, I was detailed the role of Assisting Garda by
Sergeant [NAME].

At [TIME] on [DATE], Garda [NAME, REG. NO.] arrived at [STATION / SCENE
/ LOCATION] having arrested [NAME OF SUSPECT, DOB] for an alleged
offence contrary to [SECTION/ACT].

I was directed to assist Garda [NAME] in relation to this matter.

At [TIME], I [describe assistance – e.g., helped restrain the arrested
person, witnessed cautions being administered, assisted in securing
exhibits, transported prisoner, searched the prisoner, monitored CCTV,
recorded notes, etc.].

I observed that [insert factual observations – demeanour of arrested
person, condition of exhibits, behaviour of

witnesses, etc.].

I can confirm that [NAME OF SUSPECT] was brought to [STATION/LOCATION]
at [TIME] and handed into the custody of Sergeant [NAME], Member in
Charge.

During this process I [state additional actions – e.g., remained present
in custody area, witnessed Section 4 rights being administered, assisted
in processing, signed custody record, remained with prisoner during
doctor attendance, etc.].

This concluded my direct involvement in this matter.

Signed: ________________________

Garda [FULL NAME]

[STATION]

[DATE]

t: Drunk in charge — assisting Garda (worked template)
b:
Statement of Garda [YOUR NAME], [REG NO], of [STATION],
made on [DATE].

I hereby declare that this statement is true to the best of my knowledge
and belief, and that I make it knowing that if it is tendered in
evidence I will be liable to prosecution if I state in it anything which
I know to be false or do not believe to be true.

I am a member of An Garda Síochána and I am attached to Unit C Core
Contingency, based at [STATION]. On [DAY, DATE] at
[TIME] I took up duty as the observer in patrol car [CALL SIGN],
alongside Garda [NAME], Garda [NAME], and Garda [NAME]. On [DAY, DATE] I was on mobile patrol in the
area of [LOCATION], [AREA], a public place, with Garda
[GARDA NAME], Garda [NAME], and Garda [NAME], following a
999 call at [TIME] the patrol car was parked outside [LOCATION], [AREA], a public place, while Garda [NAME] and Garda
[GARDA NAME] carried out a follow up enquiry in relation to the call we had
been assigned. I remained seated in the front passenger seat of the
patrol car with Garda [NAME] also present. A female member of the public
then approached me and informed me that she had observed a male who
appeared intoxicated enter a vehicle with the keys in his hand. I
requested Garda [NAME] to go and have a look at the vehicle whilst I
remained with the patrol car. From my position I could see Garda [NAME]
engaged in conversation with a person inside the vehicle. After a few
minutes passed, I attempted to call Garda [NAME] but received no answer.
I then drove the patrol car up to the location and exited same. I
observed a male seated in the driver’s seat of a [COLOUR MAKE MODEL],
registration [REG], which was parked outside [LOCATION],
a public place. The vehicle was on the left side of the road and was
facing in the direction of [STREET], [AREA], a public place. The
environment at the time was dark, and it had rained earlier that evening
so the ground was damp, however it was not raining at the time I
observed the male. The area was lit by street lighting which allowed me
to clearly observe the male and his condition. I also used my
Garda-issued torch to illuminate the area and further assess the man’s
state. The vehicle was positioned so that the driver’s door was directly
at the roadside. From my observations, the male appeared intoxicated.
The male was slurring his words and emitted a smelled strongly of
alcohol from his breath. I immediately removed the keys from the
ignition to prevent him from driving off and to ensure the safety of the
public. I then handed the keys to Garda [NAME] for safekeeping. Based on
my observations, I instructed Garda [NAME] to arrest the male for Section
5(1) of the Road Traffic Act 2010. I observed Garda [NAME] explain the
reason for arrest in ordinary language to the man. Garda [NAME] cautioned
the male in the usual manner, asking him did he understand the caution.
Shortly after the caution, I placed the man in handcuffs as he appeared
intoxicated and I believed it necessary to ensure my safety, the safety
of my colleagues, and that of the public. I also believed it necessary
as I thought the man may attempt to run away. The arrested person
stated: "[EXACT WORDS SPOKEN]" At
this point, Garda [NAME] and Garda [NAME] had completed their enquiry
in relation to the 999 call nearby. I left Garda [NAME] with the arrested
person and drove the patrol car to collect Garda [NAME] and Garda
[GARDA NAME], who were less than 60–80 yards away from the location. I then
quickly returned to the scene. The arrested person was placed in the
rear of the official Garda vehicle driven by Garda [NAME]. I took up
position in the front passenger seat. Garda [NAME] and Garda [NAME] both
sat in the rear of the vehicle alongside the arrested person to ensure
safe supervision during conveyance. The arrested person was then
conveyed directly to [STATION], [AREA]. This statement
has been read over and it is correct.

Signed: ___________________________

Garda [YOUR NAME], [REG NO]

Nomination Statement

Statement of evidence of Garda [FULL NAME], [REG NO] of [STATION], made
on [DATE] at [STATION].

"I hereby declare that this statement is true to the best of my
knowledge and belief and that I make it knowing that if it is tendered
in evidence I will be liable to prosecution if I state in it anything
which I know to be false or do not believe to be true."

I am a member of An Garda Síochána attached to [STATION]. On [DATE] at
[TIME] I took up duty at [LOCATION/UNIT]. While on duty, I was contacted
by Garda [NAME], who informed me that he/she was investigating an
incident and had obtained CCTV footage which may be of evidential

value. Garda [NAME] requested that I view the CCTV footage to see if I
could identify any persons shown.

At [TIME] on [DATE] I attended at [STATION/LOCATION] where Garda [NAME]
arranged for me to view the CCTV footage on a designated CCTV computer.
Garda [NAME] left the room and I was left alone to view the footage
without disturbance.

I then viewed CCTV footage from [LOCATION] on [DATE(S)]. While viewing
the footage I observed [NUMBER] person(s) walking in the direction of
the camera.

[Male 1 description: clothing, footwear, headwear, etc.]

[Male 2 description: clothing, footwear, headwear, etc.]

I immediately recognised one of the males as [FULL NAME, DOB if known,
ADDRESS/

STATUS if known]. I recognised this male through my work in An Garda
Síochána over the past [TIME PERIOD] and I would describe him as having
[DISTINCTIVE FEATURES / BUILD / MANNERISMS]. His facial features, style,
and bodily mannerisms corresponded exactly with the male I observed in
the CCTV footage.

I can say without doubt that the male I observed in the CCTV footage is
[NAME]. I did not recognise any other person in the footage.

I then stopped the CCTV and called Garda [NAME] back into the office. I
described to Garda [NAME] the clothing worn by the identified male and
confirmed that I could say without doubt that it was [NAME] in the clip
I had viewed. I made this nomination of identification orally and gave
an undertaking to later commit it to writing. I had no further dealings
with this

case. This statement is correct and has been read over by me.

Signed: _________________________

Garda [FULL NAME]

[REG NO]

An Garda Síochána

[]

[][]

Sergeant Nigel Petrie,

Unit C,

Mountjoy Garda Station.

Re: Burglary at ________________________________ - Contrary to Section
12 of the Criminal Justice (Theft and Fraud Offences) Act, 2001

Alleged Offender: _______________ (DOB ________) of
______________________

Injured Party: _______________ (DOB ________) of ______________________

Investigating: Garda ____________, Mountjoy Garda Station.

Pulse Incident: ________________

Purpose of File: e.g. for Directions/Representations

Next Court Date: Outline date and Court/state “not currently before the
Courts” – you also need to outline why it is in court e.g.
hearing/mention/directions

Introduction

This investigation file will outline an incident of _____________ which
occurred ay _____hrs on the ________________ 2025 at ____LOCATION____.
This incident took place between 13:30hrs and 14:00hrs on the 25^(th) of
January 2025. During this incident ____________brief outline of what
happened, e.g. a youth was assaulted by two male adults and received
head injuries, which required ten stiches____. The two suspects in this
case were identified, detained pursuant to Section 4 of the Criminal
Justice Act, 1984. Neither suspect made admissions during interview.
Ultimately, both suspects were charged and bailed and are next due
before Court 1 CCJ on the XX/XX/XXXX. The purpose of this file is to
seek DPP’s directions and representation in this case.

**The initial summary/introduction should be short and ideally stay on
this page**

Locus in Quo

**Example** The location of this alleged incident is
_____________________________, a public place. The road is a one way two
lane carriageway that travels from _________________ to _________ The
road is skirted on either side with a footpaths. Within the immediate
vicinity of where the alleged incident occurred is a primary school and
secondary school. The speed limit on the road is restricted to 30 km/h.
The incident occurred within the vicinity of two schools, a primary
school and a secondary school. The final resting position for the
vehicles after the collisions was immediately opposite the entrance to a
primary school.

Summary of Incident/Outline of Events

On Friday the XX/XX/XXXX Garda XXXXXX and Garda XXXXX were on duty when
they responded to a call at XXXXXXX. Upon arrival, Gardaí discovered/met
with XXXXXXXXXXX – continue to outline the incident under this
paragraph. In the case of assaults you should outline the injuries, in
the case of thefts/burglaries you need to quantify the items taken and
the value. In the case of assaults, you should create a subheading
outlining injuries.

Further Investigation of Incident

In some circumstances, additional investigation will have been carried
out after the initial incident- eg being results from Scenes of Crime
examination, additional witnesses, CCTV, have medical records been
requested/obtained, etc

Witness(es) to Incident

Where there are no witnesses, state this fact. Where there are
witnesses, complete the table below. The evidence of witness should be
short and a summary of their statement. Remember, the statement will be
read separately. – Civilian witnesses (Garda witnesses outlined later)

The following Gardaí had involvement with ___________ in the course of

Introduction:

This investigation file was prepared by Garda _________________ (S.__)
in relation to the alleged breach of Section 4 (____) Road Traffic Act,
2010 on the LOCATION on the XX/XX/202X. *Where there are other offences
outline them here by ____ALLEGED OFFENDER NAME_________, born, _______
of ____ADDRESS_____. The Alleged Offender in this case initially failed
a roadside breath test. After arrest, a blood/urine/breath sample was
obtained from ____NAME_________, which returned a result of XXXXmg or
alcohol per 100ml of breath/blood/urine. ____NAME_________ is/is not a
specified driver.

____NAME_________ is currently charged/summonsed with the following
offence(s) and is next before the Court XXXXXXX on DATE for Hearing,

-   Section 4 (____) Road Traffic Act, 2010

  This file has been prepared for the purposes of seeking representation
  by the Director of Public Prosecutions on the hearing date.

Details of Investigation:

-   Observation:

____NAME_________ was observed driving mechanically propelled vehicle
registration number ____________, a MAKE and MODEL on
_____LOCATION_________, a public place at ___hrs on the __/__/____.

-   Roadside Breath Test/MIT Checkpoint:

As part of a MIT checkpoint ____NAME_________ provided a specimen of
their breath, which resulted in a fail, which indicated the presence of
alcohol in their breath.

Having formed their opinion that ____NAME_________ may be under the
influence of an intoxicant, Garda ___________ made a demand of
____NAME_________ to provide a specimen of their breath pursuant to
Section 9(1) of the Road Traffic Act 2010. Ultimately, ____NAME_________
provided a specimen of their breath, which indicated the presence of
alcohol in their breath.

-   Arrest:

____NAME_________ was arrested on ___________________, a public place at
_____hrs on the __/__/___ after Garda _________ form his opinion.

-   Station Procedure:

Arrival at station: _____hrs on the __/__/____

Time observation Commenced: _____hrs on the __/__/____

Requirement to provide specimen of: Breath or (Blood or Urine)

The specimen provided was: Breath/Blood/Urine

Given option to retain a specimen: Chose to retain a specimen/not retain
a specimen

-   Delivery/Postage of Sample (Where applicable):

On __/__/____ at _____hrs the sealed box containing the blood/urine
specimen of ____NAME_________ was forwarded to the Medical Bureau of
Road Safety, University College Dublin by registered post, item number
_____________IE.

Results of Analysis of Blood/Urine Test

On the ___/___/______ a certificate of analysis was received from the
Medical Bureau of Road Safety stating that the blood/urine sample from
____NAME_________ contained _____ milligrammes of alcohol per 100
millilitres of blood/urine.

Results of Analysis of Breath Test

of his breath following a requirement under Section 12(1) (a) Road
Traffic Act. The result of the breath test showed the concentration of
alcohol in the breath to be ___ microgrammes of alcohol per 100
millilitres of breath.

specify if any are for drink driving or road traffic offences)

Conclusion/Recommendations

____NAME_________ having failed at a MIT checkpoint/roadside breath test
OR after Garda _____ having formed an opinion, and having provided a
breath/blood/urine specimen at the Garda Station with a result of ____
milligrammes of alcohol per 100 millilitres of breath/blood/urine is in
breach of the Road Traffic Act. Therefore I recommend that he be
prosecuted for an offence contrary to Section 4 (____) Road Traffic Act,
2010 as amended.

Please quote Ref. No: Date: XXXXXXX

Sergeant (-----Sergeant’s Name----),

Unit ___,

Mountjoy Garda Station.

RE: Breach of protection/safety/barring order which occurred on the
(----) of (----) 202(--) at (--Location/Address--).

Injured party/ Complainant: (--Name, DoB, Address--)

Suspect Offender/Accused: (--Name, DoB, Address--)

Investigating Member: (--Rank, Name,Reg No.--) Mountjoy Garda Station.

Pulse incident number: (----)

Introduction

With reference to the above, the attached file relates to one count of a
breach of protection/safety/barring (delete as appropriate), order
number (----), which occurred on the (----) of (----) 202(----) at
(--Location/Address--). The complainant, (--Complainant’s Name--)
has/has (delete as appropriate) not provided a statement in this case
and it is believe they will cooperative/not cooperate (delete as
appropriate) with a prosecution. The suspect in this case has been
charged and is currently before the Court with the next remand date
being (----) of (----) 202(----), which is a date for hearing. This file
is being submitted in order to request representation by the Chief State
Solicitor Office in order to prosecute the case.

Summary of Incident

At approximately (----)hrs on (----) of (----) 202(----) the Complainant
for the order, (--Complainant’s Name--) contacted Gardaí to make
complaint that the Accused, (--Accused Name--) for the
protection/safety/barring order had/was (--outline details, eg. Put
him/her in fear by threatening/hitting etc. a clear summary of the
breach should be outlined here--).

Gardaí arrived at the scene, (--Location/Address--) at (----)hrs. The
Complainant, (--Complainant’s Name--) (--Statement No. if applicable--)
met and spoke with Gardaí. The Complainant outlined that they had a
safety/protection/barring order against the Accused who is their
partner/ex-partner/husband/wife/other/etc.

*The Complainant produced a copy of the Order to Gardaí — or — Garda
(--Name--) confirmed that there was a safety/protection/barring order in
existence and in force.

The Complainant alleged that the Accused had (--overview eg. Been
extremely aggressive--) and was/had (--Specific details of
occurrence/breach. Eg. Shouting at her aggressively and he was now
upstairs and she was fearful he would harm her/punched and kicked them,
etc--). The Complains has/has (delete as appropriate) not provided a
statement of complaint at this time.

(--Further details of Garda actions taken should be covered in this
paragraph. Eg. Gardaí entered the premises and observed the Accused in
an intoxicated state and acting in very aggressive manner. The Accused
was shouting at and threatening to assault the Complainant and Gardaí.
The Accused was subsequently arrested for breach of the order etc. --).

Service of the Order on Accused

The order in this case was a (--safety/protection/barring--), order
number (----), which had been served on the Accused on the (----) of
(----) 202(----) by Garda (--Name of Garda--) (--Statement No. if
applicable--) at (--Location Served--). The Order in this case was
issued on the (--Date--) and is valid until the (--Date--).

Arrest of Accused/Accused

On (--day--) the (----) of (----) 202(----) at (----)hrs Garda
(--Name--) arrested the Accused on suspicion of having committed an
offence contrary to Section 33(1) of the Domestic Violence Act 2018,
which occurred on the (----) of (----) 202(----) at (--Location--). The
reason for their arrest was explained and the Accused. After caution,
the Accused (--made no comment/said the following….--), they were then
conveyed to (--Station name--) Garda Station arriving at (----)hrs. Upon
arrival that the station the Accused was introduced to the Gaoler, Garda
(--Name--) (--Statement No. if applicable--) who completed the C84
custody record in relation to the Accused.

At (----)hrs on the (----) of (----) 202(----) the Accused was charged
by Sergeant (----) as set out on charge sheet number(s) (----) and
cautioned. The Accused (--note reply or state no reply--) after caution.
The Accused was then held in Garda Custody until the next sitting of the
District Court, which was at (----)hrs on the (----) of (----) 202(----)
at the (--Specify Court eg. Criminal, Courts of Justice, Parkgate
Street, Dublin 7--). Garda (----) gave evidence of arrest, charge and
caution. Garda (----) then objected to bail/sough bail conditions. The
Accused was remanded in custody/given bail were the following conditions
were imposed,

1)  Stay away from (--Address--)

2)  Do not contact the alleged injured party/Complainant.

The case was then remanded to the (----) of (----) 202(----).

Background of Accused/Accused

The Accused, (--name--) is a (----) year old female/male who has no/XXX
previous convictions, a summary of those convictions are outlined in the
table below,

  -----------------------------------------------------------------------
  Public Order                                             X
  -------------------------------------------------------- --------------
  Criminal Damage                                          X

  Theft                                                    X

  Possession of drugs                                      X
  -----------------------------------------------------------------------

Background of Complainant/Complainant

The Complainant, (--Name--) is a (----) year old male/female. Who has
no/XXX previous convictions, a summary of those convictions are outlined
in the table below,

  -----------------------------------------------------------------------
  Theft                                                    X
  -------------------------------------------------------- --------------
  Public Order                                             X

  Possession of drugs                                      X

  Failing to appear                                        X

  Attempt to commit an indictable offence                  X

  Traffic                                                  X
  -----------------------------------------------------------------------

Relationship between the Complainant and Accused

(--give a brief summary of the relationship between the parties
involved, eg. They are mother and son and reside in the same house--)

Court Date(s)

This case was last before Court (----) on the (----) of (----) 202(----)
and was remanded to Court (----) for mention/hearing on the (----) of
(----) 202(----) at (----)hrs.

Complainant Unwilling to give Evidence/Uncooperative

All the following headings are to be addressed.

Conclusion and Recommendation

Based on the initial report, and that outlined in the statement of
Complainant (combined with the behaviour of the Accused witnessed by
Gardaí), Garda (--Name--) is of the opinion that Accused breached the
Safety/Protection/Barring Order number (----) on the (----) of (----)
202(----) at (--Location/Address--) by putting the Complainant in fear.

You should also outline wheatear in your opinion the Complainant is
unwilling/reluctant to cooperate with a prosecution that should be
outlined in this paragraph

It is Garda (----) opinion that there is sufficient evidence of the
Accused breaching the order in this case to warrant a prosecution and
secure a conviction. Accordingly, Garda (----) respectfully requests the
assistance of the Chief State Solicitors Office to prosecute this case
at the hearing date before Court (--Court Number/Location) at (--Time--)
on the (--Date--).

Forwarded for your information and onward transmission to the Chief
State Solicitors Office for representation, please.

__________________________Garda

Complainant Unwilling to give Evidence/Uncooperative

All the following headings are to be addressed.

1)  Written statement from the Complainant containing the Complainant’s
    reasons for withdrawing support or retracting the allegation

Summary to be given of the statement taken. Where no statement taken a
summary of the Complainant’s views or verbal wishes to be outlined

OR

Garda Response:

Example: On the 31st of May 2023 Garda XXXX called to 20 Main Street,
Dublin 1, spoke with the Complainant, and updated them as to the current
status of the investigation Garda XXXX requested that Complainant
provide another statement in order to clarify certain matters. The
Complainant emphatically stated that they would not provide another
statement to Gardaí, and that their statement of withdrawal still stood.

2)  Details of those with whom the Complainant has discussed the case –
    particularly anyone who has advised them (a solicitor, or community
    member or mediator, for example) and obtain their details:

Garda Response:

Example: Garda XXXX was able/unable to establish whether the Complainant
discussed the case with a third party, or whether were given advice in
relation to it.

3)  The complainant was made aware of the possible use of measures to
    assist them e.g. video link evidence, the use of a screen in court,
    accessibility to and the use of victim support services:

Garda Response:

Example: Garda XXXX has informed the Complainant of special measures
available to assist them in court; such as victim support services,
video link and a screen. The Complainant has explained that even with
these measures they would not be willing to attending court.

4)  Details as to any civil or family proceedings that have been, or are
    likely to be, commenced which may have impacted on the Complainant’s
    decision:

Garda Response:

Example: Garda XXXX was unable to establish whether there were any
ongoing civil or family proceedings which may have impacted on Ms.
Wardell’s decision.

5)  Investigating Garda’s views on the complainants withdrawal
    statement, to include the veracity of the statement, any suspicions
    of witness intimidation or pressure, and a general assessment of the
    reasons given by the complainant:

Garda Response

Example: At this time Garda XXXX does not suspect witness intimidation
has played a factor in the Complainant’s decision to withdraw their
complaint. It is Garda XXXX opinion that the withdrawal statement is a
true reflection of the Complainant’s reasons for not pursuing the
complaint; that they believe a prosecution would be in neither their nor
the Accused best interests, and ...

6)  Investigating Garda’s views on how the case should be dealt with now
    that the complainant has provided a written retraction statement,
    including proceeding with the prosecution case to Hearing, against
    the complainants wishes:

Garda Response:

Example: It is Garda XXXX’s view that there is no prima facie case in
this matter as the Complainant is the only witness in this incident, and
they are refusing to attend Court to give evidence. Therefore Garda XXXX
does not believe that the State should proceed with a prosecution in
this case.

Or

As Members of An Garda Síochána personally witnessed the breaches of the
order in question it would be the investigating member’s opinion that
the case could proceed to hearing

7)  How the complainant might react to being compelled to give evidence
    for the prosecution:

Garda Response:

Example: Garda XXXXX does not believe that the Complainant would react
well to being compelled to give evidence for the prosecution.

8)  Whether any support organisation assisting the complainant has
    expressed a view

Garda Response:

Example: Garda XXXX is not aware of any support organisations that may
have assisted the Complainant in this matter.

9)  The likely safety impact on the Complainant and any
    children/dependants of proceeding with the prosecution or indeed not
    proceeding with the prosecution

Garda Response:

Example: Garda XXXX is not in a position to accurately foresee or
predict how proceeding with the prosecution or indeed not proceeding
with the prosecution will have an impact on the actions of the
Accused/Accused and as such cannot comment on the likely safety impact
on the complainant and/or their dependent(s)/children.

Conclusion and Recommendation

Based on the initial report, and that outlined in the statement of
Complainant (combined with the behaviour of the Accused witnessed by
Gardaí), Garda (--Name--) is of the opinion that Accused breached the
Safety/Protection/Barring Order number (----) on the (----) of (----)

Sergeant ______________,

Unit ___

Mountjoy Garda Station.

Re: Sudden Death of XXXXXXX, DOB XXXXXXX of XXXXXXX on the ____ of
________ 202__.

PID- XXXXXXX

Introduction

On the ____ of ________ 202__ Garda XXXXXXX (S.___) and Garda XXXXXXX
(S.___) responded to a call for a deceased person, now known to be
XXXXXXX, born XXXXXXX at XXXXXXX. Mr/Ms. XXXXXXX had been (Outline
circumstances, eg. “discovered unresponsive by his friends John Doe and
Jack Do unresponsive. The three males had been socialising with the
night previous. Members of the Dublin Fire Brigade (DFB) attended the
scene and could not revive Mr. XXXXXXX). Doctor XXXXXXX (S.____)
pronounced death at 17.08hrs on the ____ of ________ 202__. Gardaí have
identified no suspicious circumstance surrounding the death of Mr/Ms
____ of ________ 202__.

Details of Incidents and known movements of deceased prior to death

Example - On arrival at scene Gardaí spoke with XXXXXXX (S.1) a friend
of the deceased who had discovered the body. Mr XXXXXXX told Gardaí that
the deceased was visiting his home for his birthday on the 21^(st) of
June 2024 and on arrival they had smoked a cannabis joint together
before the deceased fell asleep on the couch for the night. Mr XXXXXXX
stated that the deceased slept for the night on the couch. At
approximately 14:45hrs on the 23^(rd) of June 2024 that Mr XXXXXXX
discovered the body of the deceased on the couch unresponsive and called
for an ambulance.

Garda Actions at Scene

On arrival at scene Garda XXXXXXX spoke with members from (Outline staff
e.g. Dublin Fire Brigade/Staff at the Hospital etc) who informed him
that XXXXXXX had passed away and their body was (Outline the details of
the location of the body). Garda XXXXXXX observed the body and (Outline
actions taken, e.g.: made arrangements for XXXXXXX’s Funeral Directors
and a doctor to attend scene. Garda XXXXXXX also contacted Detective
Garda XXXXXXX to attend scene and were satisfied there were no
suspicious circumstances present).

Pronunciation of Death

Death was pronounced by Dr XXXXXXX (S.___) from (State Agency/Hospital)
at _____hrs at ______Location death pronounced________________. The
deceased body was removed from (scene) and brought to the (Location:
e.g. Dublin City Mortuary, Mortuary at the Mater Hospital etc) by
(details if known e.g Stafford’s Funeral home, hospital staff)

Details of Last Person to See Deceased Alive

XXXXXXX was the last person to see the deceased alive when they
_____(give details)____ on the __ of ________ 202___.

Identification of the Deceased

The deceased was identified to Garda XXXXXXX (S.___) at ________hrs on
the 07/06/2022 to Garda XXXXXXX at LOCATION e.g. Dublin City Mortuary.
XXXXXXX is the next of kin/mother/father/brother/wife etc of the
deceased. Subsequently, Garda XXXXXXX identified the deceased to e.g the
Senior Assistant Pathology Technician, Emma Salcidos, in the Dublin City
Mortuary.

Background of Deceased

Example - The deceased was known to Gardaí from a number of reports made
of lost property spanning from 2015 to 2021. The deceased was also a
victim of crime where Gardaí investigated an alleged Robbery where the
deceased was an injured party in the case. From speaking with the
witness’/family members, Gardaí were made aware that the deceased was a
social drug user.

Medical History of Deceased

Outline what is known. E.g. It is known that deceased had been suffering
from COPD. Gardaí are unaware of any other medical conditions the
deceased may have had.
